title: European Conquest of the Americas Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: Added six new historical timeline events covering the Laws of Burgos (1512), the Requerimiento (1513), Almagro's disastrous Chile expedition (1535–36), Landa's burning of Maya codices at Maní (1562), the Acoma Massacre (1599), and the Caste War of Yucatán (1847). Three new map points added — Tumbes (Pizarro's Peruvian landing), Acoma Pueblo (Oñate massacre site), and Tiwanaku (pre-Inca sacred center). Two new exploration routes added: Oñate's conquest of New Mexico (1598) and Cabrillo's Pacific coast expedition (1542–43). Political figures expanded with Diego de Landa and Felipe Guaman Poma de Ayala; a new KPI tracking the four surviving pre-Columbian Maya codices (of thousands destroyed) was added. Current events section updated with the February 2026 Templo Mayor discovery of a major Moctezuma I-era offering complex and a confirmed submerged Maya settlement beneath Lake Atitlán, Guatemala.

title: European Conquest of the Americas Update — Wed Feb 4, 2026
summary: Major Templo Mayor Offering Linked to Moctezuma I Uncovered — Mexico City.
